title: Incorrect Two-Pointer Approach
description: This function should check if an array can form a pair summing to target. Fix the logic error.

python.buggyCode:
def has_pair(arr, target):
    arr.sort()
    left, right = 0, len(arr)
    while left < right:
        s = arr[left] + arr[right]
        if s == target: return True
        elif s < target: left += 1
        else: right -= 1
    return False

print(has_pair([1,4,6,8], 10))

title: Wrong Carry in Addition
description: This manual digit addition has a logic error. Find it.

python.buggyCode:
def add_strings(a, b):
    i, j, carry = len(a)-1, len(b)-1, 0
    result = []
    while i >= 0 or j >= 0:
        x = int(a[i]) if i >= 0 else 0
        y = int(b[j]) if j >= 0 else 0
        total = x + y + carry
        result.append(str(total % 10))
        carry = total // 10
        i -= 1; j -= 1
    return ''.join(reversed(result))

print(add_strings('99', '1'))

c.buggyCode: // Same logic — missing carry append after loop
